global.title: ReactorBuilder uses a fluent API to wire storage, executors, read models, sync, and document models into a reactor instance
global.content: The `ReactorBuilder` from `@powerhousedao/reactor` provides a fluent API for constructing reactors: `withDocumentModels()`, `withExecutorConfig({ maxConcurrency, jobTimeoutMs })`, `withWriteCacheConfig({ maxDocuments, ringBufferSize })`, `withMigrationStrategy('auto')`, `withKysely()` (defaults to in-memory PGlite), `withQueue()` (defaults to InMemoryQueue), `withEventBus()`, `withReadModel()`, `withSync()`, and more. Calling `build()` returns `IReactor`; calling `buildModule()` returns a `ReactorModule` exposing all internal components including operationStore, keyframeStore, writeCache, documentView, documentIndexer, readModelCoordinator, processorManager, and the underlying Kysely database.
global.description: The builder pattern configures maxConcurrency, write cache, migration strategy, Kysely DB, queues, event bus, and sync for reactor construction.
